集训3 · 假期复习任务卡 · Day 14 of 24

Lake Trip Bird Project

📖 Reading

338 words · 27 target words · listen while you read

Tier 1（9词） Tier 2（7词） Tier 3（11词）

Ana was going on a summer journey to visit her grandfather, Papa Tom, by a quiet lake. Papa Tom used to be a fishing guide, and people said he was famous for knowing every fish in the water.

For her school project, Ana needed a good grade, so she wanted to write about local birds. Her idea was to hunt for facts and pictures near the lake. Papa Tom agreed to help her prepare a list of the birds that visited every morning.

When Ana arrived, the kitchen smelled wonderful, and she wanted to taste the soup bubbling on the stove before they even said hello.

On the first morning, they took the old wooden boat out onto the calm water. Ana felt a little confused about which bird was which, but Papa Tom's voice was patient and clear as he explained each one. "That one has a familiar red mark on its wing," he said.

She began to feel grateful for the quiet morning as the sun began to pour gold light across the lake. A fish jumped near them, and Ana was so surprised she almost fell in. Papa Tom laughed and said it would be foolish to worry — the lake was shallow there.

Later, Ana could swim near the shore while Papa Tom rested. The cold water made her rub her arms fast to stay warm, but she did not want to hide behind him — she wanted to enjoy the lake bravely, on her own.

That evening, Ana worked on her project. She looked at each image she had drawn and began to understand how much she had learned. She wished she could see the birds again. Her only trouble was deciding which picture to use first.

When she finished, Ana felt relieved and proud. Papa Tom read her work and smiled. "It's only fair that you feel proud of it," he said gently. Ana began to cry, not from sadness, but from happiness, wondering how she would say goodbye to this place.

🔍 Reading Comprehension

5 questions · tap to check your answer

1In "Papa Tom used to be a fishing guide, and people said he was famous for knowing every fish in the water," what does guide mean?
B — a guide is a person who shows the way, here meaning Papa Tom helped others find and understand the fish.
2In "Papa Tom laughed and said it would be foolish to worry — the lake was shallow there," what does foolish mean?
B — foolish means not sensible, here meaning it made no sense for Ana to worry since the water was shallow.
3In "Her only trouble was deciding which picture to use first," what does trouble mean?
C — trouble means a problem or difficulty, here the small challenge of choosing which picture to use.
4Why did Ana almost fall into the water?
B — the story says a fish jumped near them and Ana was so surprised she almost fell in.
5How did Ana feel when she finished her school project?
B — the story says Ana felt relieved and proud when she finished, and Papa Tom told her it was fair to feel that way.
🔁
